notes: Employer-pays, timely wage settlement, wages through formal channels; silent on remittance fees, FX, and payment channels.
Bank payment by 7th of month; employer bears recruitment costs; silent on remittance provisions.
Employment agreements mandate minimum wages and entitlements; no remittance or FX clauses.
Orientation covers worker rights and basic finances; not a formal MoU financial-literacy clause.
Korean labour law requires bank-account wage deposit; guide explains account opening.
Requires disclosure of remittance fees, FX rate, spread, charges, payout amount, and delivery time.
Off-us P2P fees must not materially differ from on-us; zero fees for micro-merchant EFTs.
